summary:
The "Florida Freeze Disaster Assistance Act" (S. 4663) appropriates funds for the Secretary of Agriculture to provide disaster relief to agricultural producers affected by freezing or cold weather conditions.

The bill appropriates $3,500,000,000 from the Treasury for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2027, as an additional amount under "Agricultural Programs—Office of the Secretary." These funds would remain available until expended.

The Secretary of Agriculture would use the money to provide assistance in the form of block grants to States to compensate for losses of revenue, quality, or production of crops (including crops prevented from being planted), trees, bushes, and vines that result from a freeze or cold weather condition. The assistance must include compensation to producers for losses of multi-year crops.

The bill places several conditions on the funds. They may be used exclusively for losses occurring in counties that received a disaster designation for freeze and related cold weather conditions issued by the Secretary on March 4, 2026. The funds become available only upon the request of a State department of agriculture or other appropriate State agency, as determined by the Secretary.

The bill requires reporting: no later than 120 days after enactment, and for each fiscal quarter thereafter until the funds are fully expended, the Secretary must submit reports to the Appropriations Committees of the House and Senate describing how the assistance is being implemented.

Finally, the appropriated amount is designated by Congress as an emergency requirement under section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985.

The bill was introduced in the Senate on June 2, 2026, by Mr. Scott of Florida and Mrs. Moody, and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
